name: Chocolate & Peanut Butter Buttons
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Place sheets of foil on countertop for cooling cookies.
2. For the cookies, combine brown sugar, shortening, water, and vanilla in a large bowl. Beat at medium speed of an electric mixer until well blended. Beat eggs into the creamed mixture.
3. Combine flour, cocoa, salt, and baking soda. Mix into the creamed mixture at low speed just until blended. Stir in chocolate chips.
4. Shape dough into 2-inch (5 cm) balls. Roll in granulated sugar. Place cookies 2 inches (5 cm) apart on an ungreased baking sheet. Press thumb gently into the center of each cookie.
5. Bake one baking sheet at a time at 375°F (190°C) for 7 to 9 minutes, or until cookies are set. Do NOT overbake. Press centers again with a small measuring spoon. Cool on the baking sheet for 2 minutes, then remove cookies and allow them to cool completely.
6. For the peanut butter cream filling, combine peanut butter and shortening in a medium bowl. Beat at low speed of an electric mixer until blended. Add icing sugar. Beat at low speed until blended. Add milk and vanilla. Beat at low speed until smooth. Fill centers of cooled cookies. Makes about 4 dozen cookies
